content.full_text:
to                                                                   June 16-92

John W Wallace
Fair Political Practices Com
596  Calif  95804

Dear John:
Per our Private Conversation
on June 16-92 would you please
send me written Answers to some
of my Questions
#1- Can Campaign Funds in Bank
account from 92 Election be used
it my FPPC Fines arising out
of Personal use Violations
#2. Does a local Volunteer Fire
Dept qualify as an Acceptable
Designation for a Donation at Surplus
Campaign Funds ?

Your Walter

6250 old SHC Road  Your Walter
Plymouth Calif 95669

[The image shows a lined notebook page with very faint, barely legible handwriting. The text is too faint and unclear to transcribe accurately without significant guessing. Most of the writing appears as light gray marks against the white lined paper, making individual words and sentences indecipherable.]

sections.question:
#1 - Can surplus campaign funds in bank account from '90 Election be used to pay F.P.P.C. Fines arising out of personal use violations?

#2 - Does a local Volunteer Fire Dept qualify as an acceptable organization for a donation of surplus campaign funds?
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether surplus campaign funds from a 1990 election can be used to pay FPPC fines for personal use violations, and whether a local volunteer fire department qualifies as an acceptable recipient for surplus campaign fund donations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No formal conclusion or advice letter structure present. Sender appears to be John W Wallace or referencing him; signature unclear but letter ends with address in Plymouth, California. No FPPC response document was provided, only the initial inquiry.

embedding.summary: A constituent writes to the FPPC requesting written answers to two questions about the permissible use of surplus campaign funds, specifically regarding payment of FPPC fines and donation eligibility to a volunteer fire department.
